theory.canonical_statement: Chapter: What Has Been Discovered: An Honest Assessment of the Trawin Zero Point Framework Primary Registry IDs: 000 (existence and uniqueness — the foundation whose truth is being assessed), 025 (conservation of topological charge — the most specific falsifiable prediction) Secondary IDs: 020–021 (winding number and characteristic class — the topological invariants underlying predictions 3 and 4) Tertiary IDs: All other IDs (this chapter assesses the epistemic status of the entire registry) Prediction 1 (Gravitational modulation at $10^{ 14}$) Source: The Elsasser attractor (IDs 0060–0085) and the TZP potential (ID 023). The gravitational coupling between the lunar tidal field and the Earth's TZP geometry produces a modulation at a rate determined by $V T(x) = \lambda e^{ d(x,p)/u T}$ evaluated at the Earth Moon system scale. The modulation amplitude $\delta G/G \sim 10^{ 14}$ is the ratio of the TZP potential to the gravitational coupling.
theory.technical_interpretation: c class — the topological invariants underlying predictions 3 and 4) **Tertiary IDs:** All other IDs (this chapter assesses the epistemic status of the entire registry) --- ## The Falsifiable Predictions and Their Registry Sources Each of the four falsifiable predictions in the chapter connects to specific registry entries: **Prediction 1 (Gravitational modulation at $10^{-14}$)** Source: The Elsasser attractor (IDs 0060–0085) and the TZP potential (ID 023). The gravitational coupling between the lunar tidal field and the Earth's TZP geometry produces a modulation at a rate determined by $V_T(x) = \lambda e^{-d(x,p)/u_T}$ evaluated at the Earth-Moon system scale. The modulation amplitude $\delta G/G \sim 10^{-14}$ is the ratio of the TZP potential to the gravitational coupling. **Prediction 2 (Gravitational wave sidebands)** Source: The TRAWIN d'Alembertian (ID 019) and the lemniscatic s In technical terms, the entry packages the selected formula as the operative carrier, the nearby equations as the progression layer, and the symbol key as the compact interpretation of variables used by the source.

It identifies the mathematical spine of the cited chapter and gives the entry a concrete equation, symbol key, and derivation chain for the four-page production layout.

dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The equation progression records V_T(x) = \lambda e^{-d(x,p)/u_T}; Q = w; \delta G/G \sim 10^{-14}; c_T \in H^2(M\setminus\{p\}, \mathbb{Z}). dictionary_sections.tzp_thesaurus:

\item Source context: the chapter prose that explains why the formula belongs in the registry.
\item TRAWIN closure: the reading of the formula through time, rotation, action, wave, integration, and normalization.
